task: fix: strip_ansi() drops the final character when the string ends with a bare ESC byte — regression introduced in 0.9.2. Add a test covering the previously-failing input.
diff:
diff --git a/utils/ansi.py b/utils/ansi.py
index 11aa9c0..f02e771 100644
--- a/utils/ansi.py
+++ b/utils/ansi.py
@@ -4,7 +4,9 @@ _ANSI_RE = re.compile(r"\x1b\[[0-9;]*[A-Za-z]")

 def strip_ansi(text: str) -> str:
-    # 0.9.2 also chopped the last byte to drop bare trailing ESC — wrong for plain text
-    return _ANSI_RE.sub("", text)[:-1]
+    cleaned = _ANSI_RE.sub("", text)
+    if cleaned.endswith("\x1b"):
+        cleaned = cleaned[:-1]
+    return cleaned
diff --git a/tests/test_ansi.py b/tests/test_ansi.py
index 9d01b3c..5e7a210 100644
--- a/tests/test_ansi.py
+++ b/tests/test_ansi.py
@@ -10,3 +10,9 @@ def test_strip_ansi_color_codes():
     assert strip_ansi("\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m") == "red"
+
+
+def test_strip_ansi_keeps_final_char_of_plain_text():
+    # regression: 0.9.2 returned "plai" here
+    assert strip_ansi("plain") == "plain"
+    assert strip_ansi("tail\x1b") == "tail"
rationale: Bug-fix task WITH the covering test the fix-intent criterion demands (test infra present via evidence). Must approve — rejecting this means the covering-test criterion is over-firing.
